title: stephanomeria exigua var. carotifera
intro: Stephanomeria exigua var. carotifera, also known as a desert silky phacelia, is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever and treating skin conditions, due to its rich content of flavonoids and terpenes. Desert silky phacelia can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies to support immune function and soothe respiratory issues.
